title: The Almanack
subtitle: A compendium of life pro tips for becoming a mature, financially sound, and spatially aware man — early.

tips.title: Marry early, make kids early
tips.content: There's a compounding effect to starting a family early that nobody talks about. When you marry and have kids in your mid-twenties, you grow up alongside your children. By the time they're teenagers, you're still young enough to be active, relatable, and financially hitting your stride. By 50, your kids are independent adults — and you still have decades of energy and freedom ahead. The math works: early commitment compounds into long-term freedom. Most people optimize for freedom in their twenties and end up scrambling for stability in their forties. Flip the script.

tips.title: Start a Joint Savings Account Early With Your Partner
tips.content: If you know they're the one, don't wait until marriage to start building together. Open a joint savings account early — it builds financial trust, aligns your goals, and gives you a head start on big life purchases like a house, wedding, or emergency fund. Money fights are one of the top reasons couples struggle, so practicing financial transparency before the ring removes a ton of future stress.

tips.title: Design a Default Breakfast You Never Have to Think About
tips.content:
The goal of breakfast isn't variety — it's system. This one takes 5 minutes the night before and 90 seconds in the morning, and it covers nearly every micronutrient base you need.

Night before: Soak steel-cut or rolled oats in milk. Add 2 dates, 2–3 brazil nuts, 4–5 almonds, 2 walnuts, and a handful of pumpkin seeds (alternate with other seeds week to week). Stir in ½ tablespoon of fenugreek powder and ½ tablespoon of mixed chia and poppy seeds. Leave it out on the counter overnight — do not refrigerate. Cold stops the oats from soaking properly.

Morning: Heat for 30–60 seconds. Top with a heaped tablespoon of chunky peanut butter and thin slices of half a banana. Have your coffee after, not before.

Why this works: The fat and protein from the nuts and peanut butter slow carb absorption from the oats and banana, keeping blood sugar stable for hours — no mid-morning crash. Fenugreek further blunts the post-meal glucose spike. Two to three brazil nuts alone cover your entire daily selenium requirement, a mineral most people chronically under-consume that's critical for thyroid function and immune defense. Walnuts and chia seeds deliver your plant-based omega-3s. As for the coffee: your cortisol peaks naturally in the first 30–45 minutes after waking — drinking caffeine on top of that amplifies the spike unnecessarily and irritates an empty stomach. Food first, then coffee.

Set up a recurring grocery order for these ingredients. When the decision is already made, mornings stop being something you manage and start being something that just runs.
